title: tetraberlinia moreliana
intro: Tetraberlinia moreliana, also known as the African teak, is a tree species native to the tropical regions of West and Central Africa. The bark and leaves of this tree have been traditionally used in folk medicine, exhibiting anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, making it a valuable resource for various health remedies. The medicinal properties of Tetraberlinia moreliana have been linked to several health benefits, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, as well as the management of diabetes and hypertension. In traditional herbal preparations, the bark and leaves of this tree are often brewed into teas or infused into oils, which are then used to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being.
